Validate a JSON-LD node's minimum required structure

Source: R/jsonld-helpers.R
validate_jsonld_node.Rd

Checks presence of @context (when top-level), @id, @type. Does NOT perform full JSON-LD 1.1 compliance checking. Use an external validator for that.

Usage

validate_jsonld_node(jsonld_node, require_context = FALSE)

Arguments

jsonld_node

A named list representing a JSON-LD node.

require_context

Logical, TRUE if this is a top-level document.

Value

A named list with elements valid (logical) and missing_fields (character vector).

Examples

good <- list(`@id` = "x", `@type` = "Y")
validate_jsonld_node(good)
#> $valid
#> [1] TRUE
#> 
#> $missing_fields
#> character(0)
#> 

bad <- list(`@id` = "x")
validate_jsonld_node(bad)
#> $valid
#> [1] FALSE
#> 
#> $missing_fields
#> [1] "@type"
#>

On this page
